records.ety: [OE. gilofre, gilofer, clove, OF. girofre, girofle, F. girofle: cf. F. girofle gillyflower, fr. girofle, Gr. <?/ clove tree; <?/ nut + <?/ leaf, akin to E. foliage. Cf. Caryophyllus, July-flower.]

defs: A name given by old writers to the clove pink (Dianthus Caryophyllus) but now to the common stock (Matthiola incana), a cruciferous plant with showy and fragrant blossoms, usually purplish, but often pink or white.
A kind of apple, of a roundish conical shape, purplish red color, and having a large core.
